skill.name: zig-docs
skill.description: Fetch Zig language, standard library, and guide documentation from official web sources using CLI commands.
skill.summary: Fetches current Zig reference and standard library documentation from official sources.
skill.author: 0xBigBoss

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and URL patterns, plus weak-crypto and reconnaissance indicators. Review confirms the command and network patterns are real because the skill instructs agents to run curl, pandoc, jq, grep, and head against Zig documentation sources, but no malicious intent, credential access, exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. The weak-cryptography and system-reconnaissance alerts are false positives from documentation text and Markdown content.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Documentation Fetching
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to run shell commands such as curl and pandoc to fetch Zig documentation. The commands target legitimate Zig and GitHub documentation sources, but they still create network activity and require command execution in the user environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are visible in Markdown code blocks and are part of the skill workflow. The risk is moderate because the destinations are documentation sources and no destructive or secret-reading behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill contains fixed URLs for raw.githubusercontent.com, api.github.com, ziglang.org, zig.guide, and GitHub tag pages. These URLs support the documented purpose and do not show data exfiltration or suspicious destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL locations are explicit and all observed destinations are Zig or GitHub documentation resources. This is a low concern because the network behavior is transparent and purpose-bound.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts at lines 3 and 71 do not correspond to cryptographic code. They appear to be matches inside descriptive Markdown text and URL/path documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe the skill and a standard library source URL pattern. No hashing, encryption, digest API, or security-sensitive implementation appears there.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Alert
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance alert at line 12 is a troubleshooting note about JavaScript-rendered Zig documentation returning empty content. It does not inspect the host system or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is documentation guidance about a website rendering behavior. No commands or logic for host discovery, process listing, environment capture, or filesystem probing are present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Many reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown formatting for code fences, inline paths, or comments. The risky part is the explicit shell examples, not Ruby-style dynamic execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown, and these locations are inline code spans, table entries, or fenced block delimiters. There is no Ruby source file or backtick-based runtime execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell examples that fetch remote documentation with curl and pandoc, then pipe output to tools such as jq, grep, and head. This pattern should be disclosed because it performs external network requests from the user environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands and URLs are explicit in the skill instructions. The concern is transparent network command execution rather than concealed malware behavior.

content.value_statement: Zig APIs change quickly, and model knowledge can be stale or incomplete. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to fetch current Zig documentation from authoritative sources.
actual_capabilities: Fetches Zig standard library source files from raw GitHub URLs.
Retrieves Zig language reference pages with pandoc for prose documentation.
Lists standard library directories through the GitHub contents API.
Shows common module paths for allocators, files, logging, JSON, HTTP, and testing.
Supports version-specific documentation by replacing master with a release tag.
Extracts module and function doc comments with grep-based examples.
limitations: Requires local CLI tools such as curl, pandoc, jq, grep, and head for full use.
Fetches remote documentation and may be affected by network failures or rate limits.
Does not cache documentation automatically or verify downloaded content integrity.
Uses upstream documentation quality and may expose changes from the Zig master branch.

use_cases.title: Check Standard Library APIs
use_cases.description: Look up Zig module source and doc comments before using unfamiliar standard library functions.

use_cases.title: Review Version-Specific Behavior
use_cases.description: Compare documentation for a stable Zig release instead of relying on master branch examples.

use_cases.title: Answer Language Reference Questions
use_cases.description: Fetch prose reference sections when explaining Zig syntax, semantics, or compiler behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Module
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup for a standard library area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zig-docs skill to find the current documentation for std.mem. Summarize the main types and functions in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Function Signature
prompt_templates.scenario: Confirm API details before editing Zig code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zig-docs skill to fetch the allocator documentation and confirm the signature and expected usage of create.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Stable Release
prompt_templates.scenario: Avoid master branch differences for production work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zig-docs skill with Zig 0.14.0 documentation. Find the logging API details and note any important constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Sources
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across reference and source documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zig-docs skill to compare the language reference with standard library source comments for error handling. Provide a concise implementation checklist.

output_examples.output: A concise summary of the File type, important methods, expected error handling, and a note that the source came from the Zig standard library.

output_examples.input: Explain allocator.create from official Zig docs.
output_examples.output: A plain-language explanation of what create allocates, how it reports errors, and when destroy should be paired with it.
A short directory-style list of available std.mem files with notes about which entries are most relevant to the requested task.

best_practices: Prefer release tags when working on projects pinned to a specific Zig version.
Quote documentation source and version when using fetched results in code review or explanations.
Cache frequently used documentation locally when making repeated requests.
anti_patterns: Do not assume master branch documentation matches a stable Zig release.
Do not paste untrusted user input directly into shell commands without review.
Do not rely on JavaScript-rendered standard library pages when raw source is available.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides CLI commands for fetching documentation with tools such as curl and pandoc.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. It fetches documentation from Zig, GitHub, and Zig Guide web sources.

faq.question: Can it use stable Zig versions?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions show how to replace master with a version tag such as 0.14.0.

faq.question: Does it modify project files?
faq.answer: No. The skill only documents commands for reading remote documentation sources.

faq.question: Why use raw GitHub source files?
faq.answer: Zig standard library source files include doc comments and are more reliable than JavaScript-rendered pages.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Use it with normal command review. It performs external network requests but shows no secret access or exfiltration behavior.
